title: course_cdeg not validated in decoder - accepts values > 35999
description:
File: python/src/lichen/gateway/compact_cot.py
Lines: 253, 166-168

The decoder reads course as a uint16 (0-65535):
```python
course = struct.unpack('>H', data[11:13])[0]
```

But course_cdeg should only be 0-35999 (0-359.99 degrees). The encoder normalizes course to [0, 360) via modulo at line 774, producing centidegrees 0-35999. However, a malformed message with course_cdeg=40000 (400 degrees) would:

1. Be decoded without error
2. Produce course_deg = 400.0 via the property at lines 166-168
3. Be semantically invalid (bearing must be 0-360)

Impact: Invalid bearing data could confuse ATAK or downstream systems.

Fix: Validate in decoder:
```python
if course > 35999:
    raise DecodeError(f'Course {course} centidegrees exceeds 359.99 degrees')
```

Or normalize on decode (less strict):
```python
course = course % 36000  # Normalize to valid range
```
